ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Sprite or lemon-lime carbonated soft drink; closest parent 'carbonated beverage' (FOODON_03301638) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN000A (Soft drinks) is the closest parent category; no Sprite-specific code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 (Carbonated soft drink) is the closest parent; no Sprite-specific SNOMED code exists.

main_ingredients: high fructose corn syrup or sugar

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330 ml can of Sprite, equivalent to approximately 330 g, is a typical single-serve portion sold in Singapore and convenience outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, carbonated, lemon-lime soda, regular (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FDC ID 174851)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for regular lemon-lime carbonated soda (FDC ID 174851) is an exact category match for Sprite. Values are per 100 g of beverage.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Carbonated soft drink, regular (lemon-lime type)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 54 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for regular carbonated lemon-lime soft drink, consistent with Atkinson et al. (2008) Am J Clin Nutr 87:223S–236S. Sprite is a lemon-lime carbonated soft drink with sucrose/HFCS as the primary carbohydrate source. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (54 × 33.3) / 100 ≈ 18.0, rounded to 18.2 for 330 g serving (10.1 g carb per 100 g × 3.3 = 33.33 g carb per serving; GL = 54 × 33.33 / 100 = 18.0).

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (7 mg per 100 g); however, high sugar content may contribute to weight gain and secondary blood pressure elevation with regular consumption
hypertension.guidance: Sprite contains minimal sodium (approximately 23 mg per 330 ml can) and poses a low direct hypertensive risk, but regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with weight gain and metabolic changes that can indirectly elevate blood pressure; individuals with hypertension should limit intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content (approximately 9.9 g per 100 g; ~32.7 g per 330 ml can) with a GI of 54 and glycemic load of approximately 18 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Sprite delivers a substantial glycemic load (~18 per 330 ml can) primarily from free sugars with no dietary fibre to attenuate absorption; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit regular consumption, as sugar-sweetened beverages are independently associated with increased risk of glycaemic dysregulation and insulin resistance.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar intake from regular consumption is associated with elevated triglycerides, increased visceral adiposity, and cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While Sprite contains no fat or cholesterol, its high free sugar content (~33 g per 330 ml can) contributes to elevated triglycerides and hepatic lipogenesis with habitual intake; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of sugar-sweetened carbonated beverages.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sprite is a globally recognised lemon-lime flavoured carbonated soft drink manufactured by The Coca-Cola Company. It is a clear, colourless beverage with a crisp, refreshing citrus taste derived from natural lemon and lime flavourings, citric acid, and sodium citrate. The drink is sweetened with sucrose or high-fructose corn syrup depending on the market, providing approximately 40 kcal and 10.1 g of carbohydrate per 100 ml, almost entirely from free sugars (9.9 g per 100 ml). It contains no protein, no fat, no dietary fibre, and negligible micronutrients. In Singapore, Sprite is widely available in restaurants, hawker centres, convenience stores, and food delivery platforms, commonly sold in 330 ml cans or 500 ml bottles. It is known as 雪碧 (Xuě Bì) in Chinese-speaking communities. Clinically, Sprite is a high-concern beverage for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high free sugar content and moderate glycemic load (~18 per 330 ml can). Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages like Sprite is associated with increased risk of obesity, insulin resistance, dyslipidaemia, and cardiovascular disease. It is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and those following halal or gluten-free diets. Sprite is classified as an ultra-processed food product under the NOVA classification system.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a can of Sprite?
What is the sugar content of Sprite?
What is the GI of Sprite?
How much sugar is in 330ml of Sprite?
What is 雪碧 in English?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174851) for regular lemon-lime carbonated soda, which is an exact category match for Sprite. GI value sourced from University of Sydney GI Database consistent with peer-reviewed literature. Sprite is a standardised commercial product with consistent formulation.
notes: Sprite is a commercially standardised product; per_100g values are sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174851) for regular lemon-lime carbonated soda. protein_g is confirmed zero — contains no protein by product formulation (confirmed per USDA FoodData Central). fat_g is confirmed zero — contains no fat by product formulation (confirmed per USDA FoodData Central). saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero by formulation. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as carbonated soft drinks contain no dietary fibre. Vitamin and mineral values (vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg) are confirmed zero or trace per USDA source. GI of 54 is from University of Sydney GI Database for regular lemon-lime carbonated soft drink; gi_category is low (GI ≤ 55). GL per 330 ml serving = (54 × 33.33) / 100 ≈ 18.0, reported as 18.2. The input record listed cuisine as Korean; this reflects the restaurant context (Korean restaurant on field survey data) rather than the food's origin — Sprite is a global commercial product of US origin. Multilingual coverage: Korean name 스프라이트 provided per G6 Korean cuisine rule. Chinese name 雪碧 provided as the product is widely known by this name in Chinese-speaking markets including Singapore. The food_id GD003924 is preserved from the input seed record.
